icons.name: x402 Protocol
icons.description: An HTTP-native payment standard enabling AI agents to pay for resources inline using HTTP 402 status codes without redirects or human intervention.

icons.name: Agent Wallet
icons.description: A cryptographically secured spending capability held by an AI agent, enabling it to autonomously authorize and execute financial transactions.

icons.name: Machine-to-Machine Commerce
icons.description: Fully autonomous economic transactions between AI agents without human intermediation, forming the basis of an agent-native economy.

icons.name: Auth.md
icons.description: An open agent authentication protocol using markdown-based identity documents to grant verifiable, revocable credentials to AI agents.

icons.name: Context as a Service
icons.description: An infrastructure layer that dynamically assembles, manages, and delivers contextual information to AI agents on demand at inference time.

icons.name: Active Graph Runtime
icons.description: An event-sourced, forkable agent runtime where state is a live graph of nodes and edges, enabling branching, replay, and parallel execution.

icons.name: Agent Harness
icons.description: A production control cage that constrains, monitors, and governs AI agent behavior, enforcing policy boundaries and capturing execution traces.

icons.name: Lethal Trifecta
icons.description: The dangerous security combination when an AI agent has access to private data, processes untrusted content, and can communicate externally — enabling data exfiltration.

icons.name: Tokenmaxxing
icons.description: The practice of maximizing useful work extracted per token consumed, optimizing prompt density, context efficiency, and output quality per token cost.

icons.name: MCP Apps
icons.description: User interfaces and interactive applications returned directly from MCP tool calls, enabling agents to surface rich UI components inline in responses.

icons.name: Agent Supply Chain Security
icons.description: Provenance tracking and integrity verification through multi-agent pipelines, ensuring trust and auditability from data ingestion through action execution.

icons.name: Self-Improving Software Factory
icons.description: A recursive loop where AI agents write, test, and deploy code that improves the agents themselves, creating compounding autonomous software development.

icons.name: Agentic Commerce Stack
icons.description: The complete layered protocol stack enabling autonomous purchasing — spanning identity, payment rails, policy enforcement, and settlement for AI agents.

icons.name: Forward-Deployed Engineering
icons.description: Embedding software engineers directly with customers to build, iterate, and deploy AI systems live in production alongside business stakeholders.

icons.name: Gradient-Free Continual Learning
icons.description: Model adaptation techniques that update learned behavior through inference-time mechanisms rather than backpropagation, enabling deployment-time learning.

icons.name: Dual-Surface Architecture
icons.description: A system design where the same underlying tool and capability layer serves both human-facing UI surfaces and agent-facing API surfaces simultaneously.

icons.name: Agent Budget
icons.description: Governance controls that define and enforce spending limits, resource caps, and authorization thresholds for autonomous AI agent transactions.

icons.name: Autoresearch
icons.description: AI agents that autonomously design, run, and interpret scientific or ML experiments, compressing the research cycle without human direction.

icons.name: Computer Use 2.0
icons.description: Next-generation browser automation with multiple parallel cursors enabling AI agents to operate many browser sessions concurrently on the same machine.

icons.name: Homa Network Protocol
icons.description: A low-latency datacenter transport protocol designed as an RDMA replacement to maximize inference throughput and minimize tail latency in AI clusters.

icons.name: P/D Disaggregation
icons.description: Separating the prefill and decode phases of LLM inference onto specialized hardware instances to optimize throughput and resource utilization independently.

icons.name: KV Cache-Aware Routing
icons.description: Intelligent inference request routing that directs queries to the instance holding the most relevant KV cache state, minimizing recomputation and latency.

icons.name: Eval-Driven Development
icons.description: A software methodology where evaluations are the primary development loop — writing evals before code and using eval results to drive all engineering decisions.

icons.name: Agent Bytecode
icons.description: Domain-specific intermediate languages designed as substrates for AI agents, enabling portable, verifiable, and optimizable agent programs across runtimes.
